== Decoding a JWT token

=== JWT tools
The upcoming activities require reading, modifying and tampering with JWTs. webWolfLink:WebWolf[target=jwt] works great
for all of this, but if you want to challenge yourself and use tools for real penetration tests, check out the following:

- https://www.jwt.io/[JWT.io web editor]
- https://github.com/ticarpi/jwt_tool[jwt_tool Python script] (*Works great for performing JWT attacks*)
- https://portswigger.net/burp/documentation/desktop/testing-workflow/vulnerabilities/session-management/jwts[Burp
  Suite's JWT tools and plugin] (*Most commonly used in pentests*)
  - https://www.zaproxy.org/blog/2020-09-03-zap-jwt-scanner/[ZAP JWT add-on]


=== Assignment
Let's try decoding a JWT token, for this you can use the webWolfLink:JWT[target=jwt] functionality inside WebWolf or any
of the additional tools.

.Given the following token:
[source]
----
eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9..9lYaULTuoIDJ86-zKDSntJQyHPpJ2mZAbnWRfel99iI
----

Copy and paste the following token and decode the token, can you find the user inside the token?
